name: devops-days-krakow-2026
description: In modern DevOps workflows, we usually do not build applications by logging into a machine and changing things by hand. We use Dockerfiles, Kubernetes manifests, Terraform, CI/CD pipelines, and other declarative tools to describe what we want the system to look like. But the operating system underneath our containers is still often treated differently: installed interactively, changed over time, patched manually, and debugged through SSH until every node becomes a little bit unique.
author: Jan Bronicki <jan.bronicki.flatcar@gmail.com>

scripts.build: mkdir -p build && marp --pdf --html --allow-local-files --no-stdin slides.md -o build/slides.pdf  --theme-set themes
scripts.html: mkdir -p build && marp --html --allow-local-files --no-stdin slides.md -o build/slides.html --theme-set themes
scripts.pptx: mkdir -p build && marp --pptx --html --allow-local-files --no-stdin slides.md -o build/slides.pptx --theme-set themes
